title: Mini pavlovas
subtitle: Individual meringue nests with whipped cream and fresh fruit
excerpt: Mini pavlovas are individually piped or spooned meringue nests, baked at the same low temperature as a large pavlova but for less time. Each one has its own crisp shell and soft centre. They are easier to serve than a large pavlova and let guests choose their own topping.
sourceNotes: Mini pavlovas are a serving adaptation of the full-size pavlova, common in British restaurant and catering contexts from the 1990s onward. The method is identical to a standard pavlova; only the portion size and bake time change. This recipe follows the established British pavlova technique.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Bake the meringue nests up to 2 days ahead and store in an airtight container at room temperature. Fill just before serving.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: conventional oven only; small meringues dry faster than a large pavlova

glossaryTerms.definition: For evenly sized mini pavlovas, mark 8 to 10 cm circles on the baking paper with a glass and use them as guides. Pipe or spoon the meringue in a spiral to fill the circle, then build up the edges with a second ring to create a shallow nest. Without a piping bag, a dessert spoon forms the shape; use the back of the spoon to drag the edges upward.

content.text: The meringue mixture is the same as a large pavlova; the difference is in how you

and how long you bake them. Small meringues need a lower oven temperature (110 degrees C rather than 120 degrees C) and less time. They are also more stable than a large pavlova: the baked shells store for 2 days in an airtight tin, so you can get them done ahead of time.

Heat the oven to 110 degrees C (conventional, not fan). Line two large baking trays with baking paper and draw eight 10 cm circles as guides. Whisk the 4 egg whites and quarter teaspoon cream of tartar to soft peaks. Add the 220 g caster sugar one tablespoon at a time on high speed until the meringue is thick and glossy. Fold in the 1 tsp cornflour and 1 tsp vinegar. Divide the meringue between the circles, building up the edges to create a shallow well in each.

Bake for 40 to 45 minutes until the outside is dry and the nests lift easily from the paper without sticking. Turn off the oven and leave the meringues inside with the door slightly open for at least 1 hour. Store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 days if not using straight away.

Place the meringue nests on individual plates or a serving board. Spoon or pipe the lightly whipped cream into each hollow. Arrange the berries on top and drizzle over any passion fruit pulp. Serve immediately.

items.solution: Draw circles on the baking paper as guides. Trace around a small bowl or glass (about 10 cm diameter) with a pencil, then flip the paper before adding the meringue so the pencil marks face down.

items.problem: Shells are sticky rather than dry on the outside
items.solution: They needed more time in the oven, or the kitchen is very humid. A 5 to 10 minute extension at the same temperature should dry the surface. Do not increase the temperature or the outsides will colour and crack.
